challenges.title: Number Converter
challenges.description: Create three variables: 'hex_val' storing the integer value of hex '0xFF', 'oct_val' storing the integer value of octal '0o77', and 'bin_val' storing the integer value of binary '0b1010'.
challenges.starter_code:
# Convert these literal representations to integers
hex_val = # your code here
oct_val = # your code here
bin_val = # your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert hex_val == 255, f'hex_val should be 255, got {hex_val}'
assert oct_val == 63, f'oct_val should be 63, got {oct_val}'
assert bin_val == 10, f'bin_val should be 10, got {bin_val}'
hints: Use 0xFF for hex, 0o77 for octal, 0b1010 for binary
Python evaluates these to regular integers automatically

challenges.title: Division Trio
challenges.description: Given a = 17 and b = 5, compute three values: 'true_div' (true division), 'floor_div' (floor division), and 'remainder' (modulo). Store each in the named variable.
challenges.starter_code:
a = 17
b = 5

true_div = # your code here
floor_div = # your code here
remainder = # your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert true_div == 3.4, f'true_div should be 3.4, got {true_div}'
assert floor_div == 3, f'floor_div should be 3, got {floor_div}'
assert remainder == 2, f'remainder should be 2, got {remainder}'

challenges.title: Power and Round
challenges.description: Calculate 2 raised to the power of 10 and store it in 'power_result'. Then calculate the square root of 144 using ** and store it in 'sqrt_result'. Finally, round 3.14159 to 2 decimal places and store in 'rounded'.
challenges.starter_code:
# 2 to the power of 10
power_result = # your code here

# Square root of 144 (hint: x ** 0.5)
sqrt_result = # your code here

# Round 3.14159 to 2 decimal places
rounded = # your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert power_result == 1024, f'power_result should be 1024, got {power_result}'
assert sqrt_result == 12.0, f'sqrt_result should be 12.0, got {sqrt_result}'
assert rounded == 3.14, f'rounded should be 3.14, got {rounded}'
hints: Square root is the same as raising to 0.5: 144 ** 0.5
